mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate winners from losers with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and builder demand, driving negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year window, or forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of co-declining periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that outpaces rate hikes, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in >50% of tightening periods with rising industrial production, or the regime filter adds no predictive alpha over a baseline model.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding 3.0% annually forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >58% of high-inflation observations, or the threshold fails to produce a statistically significant return differential.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a Rank IC below 0.01 over a 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of joint tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP growth sustains corporate revenue expectations, mechanically lifting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month through improved earnings forecasts.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with quarterly GDP growth rates.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production dropping below its 12-month moving average signals a supply-side contraction that triggers immediate risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.2 over a 3-year evaluation period.
